bids.reasoning: The negotiation has just begun (t=0.0). The opponent's initial offer provides very low utility for me on all issues. As it is my first counter-offer, I will propose the combination of values that maximizes my utility across the board to set a favorable anchor.

The opponent has repeated their opening offer with no concessions, signaling a firm stance. Since the time fraction is still extremely early (t=0.02), I am in no hurry to make drastic changes. I will hold firm on my highly weighted issues (A, B, and C) while making a minor concession on issueD to see if the opponent becomes willing to negotiate.

The opponent is repeating the exact same low-utility offer (providing me a utility of ~0.25). Since it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.04), I will maintain a high-utility position to anchor the negotiation. I will slightly concede on my lowest-weight issue, issueE, to see if the opponent is willing to reciprocate movement.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.06). The opponent remains completely inflexible, repeatedly proposing the same complete set of 'valueE' across all issues. I will slightly adjust my offer on issueB (a higher weight issue) to test if they are willing to deviate from their initial stance, while maintaining my top preferences on issueA, issueC, issueD, and issueE.

The current time fraction is very low (t=0.08). The opponent has repeatedly bid the exact same offer (valueE for all issues), which has an extremely low utility for me. Since we are still in the very early stage of negotiation, I will continue to propose offers that are highly favorable to me while making minor concessions to probe the opponent's willingness to engage. I will hold firm on issueA and issueB, but offer a new value for issueC to see if it elicits a different response.

The opponent is repeating the exact same low-utility offer ("valueE" on all issues) across every turn. Since the time fraction is still very early (t=0.1), I will maintain a high-utility position overall but will signal a willingness to compromise by offering "valueE" on issueE. This issue is of relatively low importance to me (10% weight) but clearly something they are asking for, while allowing me to preserve my top utility choices on the heavier-weighted issues.

The opponent is repeating their opening offer which has extremely low utility for me (around 0.25) across all issues. Since the time fraction is still very early (t=0.12), I will not concede much utility, but I will continue proposing different high-utility configurations (above 0.85) to explore whether the opponent responds better to concessions on specific minor issues, such as issueD. I will hold firm on my highest-priority issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC).

The opponent continues to repeat their opening offer consisting completely of 'valueE' for all issues, taking an extremely firm position. As it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.14), I will maintain high utility on my most important issues (issueA, issueB, issueC). However, I will offer the opponent 'valueE' for issueD—which carries low weight for me but appears critical to them based on their consistent demands—while finding a highly mutually beneficial trade-off on the rest.

The opponent has repeatedly proposed the exact same offer (`valueE` for all issues) since the beginning without any variation or concessions. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.16), I will maintain my top preferences for my most heavily weighted issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) to protect my core utility. However, to encourage movement, I will concede completely to their requested `valueE` on my two lowest-weight issues (issueD and issueE).

The opponent has finally shifted from their rigid initial position of 'valueE' across all issues by changing issueC to 'valueD'. Given that it is still very early in the negotiation (t=0.18), I want to maintain a high utility for my top issues while signaling that I noticed and am responsive to their concession. I will match their offer on issueC to 'valueD' while keeping my other issues at my most preferred values, yielding an overall utility of 0.81.

The opponent has repeatedly requested `valueE` for all issues but recently showed some flexibility by conceding `issueC` to `valueD`. I will accept their request for `issueC: valueD` since my utility for it is acceptable (0.55) and it helps to lock in early mutual agreement on an issue. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.2), I will hold firm on the issues I care about most (`issueA` and `issueB`) while exploring slight concessions on lower-weight issues like `issueD`.

The opponent continues to hold firm on their offer for issues A, B, D, and E (asking for valueE) while keeping their recent concession on issueC to valueD. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.22), I want to signal a willingness to compromise by exploring slightly different values for my lowest-weight issues (issueD and issueE) while holding firm on my most important issues (issueA and issueB), maintaining a high utility around 0.76.

The negotiation is still in its early stages (t=0.24). I see the opponent has made a minor concession on issueC (to valueD), bringing my utility on that issue up, but remains fixated on valueE for everything else. I will match their concession on issueC and offer valueE on issueE, which has low importance to me, but I will hold firm on my most highly valued issues (issueA, issueB, and issueD) to keep my total utility high while opening up potential tradeoffs.

The opponent has rigidly requested `valueE` for almost all issues, but recently conceded on `issueC` by asking for `valueD`. I will match their request on `issueC` (where I still get decent utility) and now concede on `issueD` by offering their preferred `valueE`, as my weight for `issueD` is very low. This allows me to hold firm on my most important issues, `issueA` and `issueB`, targeting a strong utility while showing willingness to compromise.

The opponent has remained rigid on most issues but recently conceded issueC to valueD and issueD to valueD. I will accept their proposal for issueC, as it provides me a decent utility of 0.55 on a moderately important issue. To maintain my overall utility near ~0.77 in this early stage (t=0.28), I will propose a new value for issueD (valueC) while holding firm on my most important issues, issueA and issueB.

The opponent is holding extremely firm, exclusively bidding `valueE` for nearly all issues, but has recently introduced `valueD` for `issueC` and `issueD`. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.3), I want to maintain a high utility profile (above 0.80) while testing whether incorporating the opponent's newly suggested values will elicit further concessions. I will offer `valueD` for `issueD` to mirror their latest concession, while maintaining my highly preferred values for `issueA`, `issueB`, `issueC`, and `issueE`.

The current time fraction is 0.32, which allows me to maintain a relatively high utility target of roughly 0.72. The opponent has repeatedly signaled interest in `valueD` for `issueC` and `issueD`, so I am including those values in this bid as a concession since my weight on `issueD` is low and `valueD` for `issueC` yields decent utility. I will hold firm on both `issueA` and `issueB` at their maximum utility values (`valueA`), as they are my highest-weighted issues and must be protected this early in the negotiation.

The opponent is gradually conceding, having moved to 'valueD' on issues C, D, and A. At this early-to-mid stage (t=0.34), I will propose a significant compromise by fully accepting their current requested values for issues C, D, and E (which are valueD, valueD, and valueE). In exchange, I will hold firm on issues A and B, which are my highest-weighted priorities, ensuring my overall utility remains protected while moving aggressively toward agreement.

The negotiation is still early at t=0.36. I observe the opponent is progressively shifting their stances from 'valueE' to 'valueD', most noticeably on issueA, issueC, and issueD. I will accept their shift to 'valueD' on issueC (which gives me moderate 0.55 utility), but I must hold firm on my highest-priority issues (issueA and issueB) where their latest demands yield very low utility for me. I am exploring alternative options on issueD and issueE to find a common ground while maintaining my overall utility above 0.7.

The opponent has slightly shifted their demands to include `valueD` for `issueA` and `issueD`, while maintaining their long-standing preferences for `issueB: valueE`, `issueC: valueD`, and `issueE: valueE`. Since we are approaching the middle of the negotiation, I will hold firm on my highest-priority `issueA` to protect my baseline utility. However, to signal flexibility and progress toward an agreement, I will maintain my previous concessions on `issueC` and `issueE` while introducing a slight concession on `issueB` (from valueA to valueB) and proposing a middle-ground value for `issueD`.

The opponent has made minor concessions, moving to 'valueD' on issueA, issueC, and issueD. Since my focus is on maintaining a solid utility while working towards an agreement by the deadline, I will adopt their preferred value 'valueD' on issueC and issueD, as my utility on issueD matters less compared to my high-priority issues. I am also offering a slight concession on issueB (valueB) to see how they respond while firmly maintaining issueA at my highest utility value 'valueA'.

The opponent has consistently proposed `valueD` for `issueC` and `issueD`, and I am willing to meet them there since I can secure a reasonable utility on those issues or compensate with others. My targeted utility is around 0.72. I will match their demands on `issueC` and `issueD` while holding firm on `issueA` and `issueB` where my importance weights are highest.

The current time is 0.44. The opponent has started making small concessions (now proposing valueD for issueA and issueD). I still need to keep my utility relatively high considering we are not yet halfway to the deadline. I will maintain my top values for my most critical issues (issueA and issueB), but I will include issueC: valueD and issueE: valueE to align with the opponent's previous requests, exploring issueD: valueB to find mutually acceptable compromises.

The opponent has consistently requested `valueD` for `issueC` and `valueE` for `issueE`, remaining largely unyielding across their recent offers. To show flexibility at this intermediate stage of the negotiation (t=0.46), I will accept those values, which still provide me with a reasonable baseline. Meanwhile, I will propose a slight concession on `issueD` to `valueC` to test for mutual gains, while maintaining my target on my two most critical issues, `issueA` and `issueB`.

The negotiation is nearing the halfway point (t=0.48), and my utility goal remains around 0.70-0.75 as I still have room to explore tradeoffs. The opponent's offers have shifted to include `issueC: valueD`, `issueD: valueD`, and `issueA: valueD`, signaling clear preferences. I will match their preference on `issueC: valueD` since my utility for it is decent, but keep my highest-weight issue (`issueA`) at my top value, while conceding slightly on `issueB` (to `valueB`) to see if it brings us closer to an agreement.

We have reached the midpoint of the negotiation (t=0.5). The opponent has shown consistency in requesting 'valueD' for issueC, 'valueD' for issueD, and 'valueE' for issueE. Since my weights for issueD and issueE are relatively low and my utility for 'valueD' on issueC is acceptable (0.55), I will proactively concede to their exact requested values on these three issues to break the deadlock. In exchange, I am holding completely firm on issueA and issueB to secure a utility of ~0.66, reflecting a fair mid-game compromise.

The opponent is holding firm on issueB and issueE while conceding on issueA, issueC, and issueD. Since we are around the mid-game mark (t=0.52), I will maintain my highest utility values on my most important issues (issueA and issueB), but perfectly match the opponent's recent demand for issueC (valueD) and issueD (valueD). For issueE, I am reverting to my highest-utility value (valueB) to sustain a reasonable total utility (~0.73) before making larger concessions closer to the deadline.

We are just past the halfway mark (t=0.54). The opponent has shown consistency in their offers for issueC (valueD), issueD (valueD), and issueE (valueE). I will accept these three values as a concession, since they are relatively tolerable for me (especially issueC: valueD), but I will drop slightly on issueB (from valueA to valueB) to show flexibility while holding firm on issueA, my most important issue, to ensure my overall utility stays above 0.60.

The current time fraction is slightly past the midpoint (0.56). The opponent has shown strong adherence to `valueE` for issues B and E, and slowly conceded on issueC (`valueD`), issueD (`valueD`), and issueA (`valueD`). I will accommodate their preference on issues C and E where my utility loss is acceptable, slightly compromise on issueD to `valueC`, and maintain `valueA` and `valueB` for my highest-weighted issues A and B to protect my utility (targeting ~0.64).

The opponent has consistently requested `valueE` and `valueD` for their highest-priority issues, particularly shifting to `valueD` for `issueC` and `issueD`. Since we are at `t=0.58`, I will offer concessions on the lower-weighted issues (`issueC` and `issueD`) by matching their requested `valueD` for both, which they have signaled they want. However, since my weight for `issueA` and `issueB` is much higher (totaling 0.5), I will firmly hold onto my high utilities for those issues to maintain a viable deal value while continuing to search for a tradeoff.

The opponent is holding extremely firm on `issueE: valueE` and `issueB: valueE`, while moving slightly on other issues to their second preferences (like `issueC: valueD` and `issueD: valueD`). To test their flexibility while still guarding my highest-weight issues, I will concede fully to their long-standing requests on `issueC`, `issueD`, and `issueE` (using their early preference `valueE` for `issueD`, which is slightly better for my utility than `valueD`). I will hold firm on `issueA` and concede slightly on `issueB`, targeting a utility of ~0.61 as we move into the late mid-game.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer (`issueA: valueD, issueB: valueE, issueC: valueD, issueD: valueD, issueE: valueE`) since t=0.32, which unfortunately evaluates to a very low utility for me. To encourage variation while remaining constructive, I will continue to match their preference on `issueC` (`valueD`) but experiment with a new concession on `issueB` (`valueC`) and `issueD` (`valueB`) to try and find a middle ground without compromising my most critical issue (`issueA`).

With the current time at t=0.64, I observe that you have conceded on issueA to valueC, but are holding extremely firm on your choices for the other issues. I am strictly holding my ground on issueA, which is my highest priority. However, to find a compromise, I am offering valueD on issueD (which you have continually asked for) and valueD on issueC (a concession you accepted previously), while requesting moderate options for the rest to keep my overall utility balanced.

Utility target is maintained around 0.70 as we enter the mid-to-late stage (t=0.66). The opponent has just conceded on issueA from valueD to valueC, indicating some flexibility. I will hold firm on issueA (my highest priority) but continue accommodating their preference on issueC (valueD) while offering intermediate-value compromises on issues B, D, and E to progress toward an agreement.

The opponent has recently made minor concessions on issue A (shifting from valueE to valueD to valueC). Since issue A is my highest weight (0.3), I want to hold my preferred position on it (valueA) while making concessions on issues of lower importance to me. I will offer a combination where I fully concede issues C, D, and E to the opponent's preferred options, while lowering my demand on issue B to valueC, maintaining a moderate overall utility of around 0.58.

The opponent has stubbornly held on to `valueE` for `issueB` and `issueE` for the entire negotiation, suggesting these are very high priority for them. I will fully concede to `valueE` on both `issueB` and `issueE` to secure their agreement. In exchange, to balance my own utility, I will counter by claiming my top values on `issueA`, `issueC`, and `issueD`, achieving a targeted tradeoff that maintains my utility around 0.68 as we approach the late-game.

The negotiation is entering the late game (t=0.72) and the opponent consistently demands valueD for issueC and issueD, and valueE for issueE. While I have accommodated their requests on issueC and issueD, giving them valueE on issueE requires me to maintain higher value options on my highest-priority issues, issueA and issueB. I will offer a compromise that includes their preferred values for issueC and issueD, while claiming valueA/valueB/valueA on my remaining issues to keep my overall utility healthy (around 0.65) and signal flexibility by mixing up my non-critical issues.

The current time fraction is 0.74, so we are entering the late stages of negotiation. The opponent has made small concessions on issueA, issueC, and issueD, but has remained entirely rigid on their demand for valueE on issueB and issueE. Since my utility for their requested valueE on issueB is exceedingly low, I will maintain firm on issueB (valueA) while making my first major concession on my highest-weight issue, issueA (moving from valueA to valueB). This maintains my overall utility around 0.56 while adopting their values for issues C, D, and E.

The opponent has finally conceded on issueA, offering valueB (which aligns nicely with my high priorities). However, they are remaining rigid on issues C, D, and E by asking for valueD and valueE. Since the deadline is approaching, I will accept their rigid demands on C and D, and meet them at valueB for issueA, but I will counter by asking for a slight compromise on issueB (valueB) and issueE (valueB) to maintain a decent utility above 0.55.

The opponent just accepted my concession on `issueA` to `valueB`. To reciprocate and move closer to an agreement as we approach the final stages of the negotiation, I will concede on `issueE` by moving to their preferred `valueE`. I will maintain `issueB` at `valueB` to preserve a balanced overall utility and finalize an equitable deal.

The opponent has made significant concessions on issueA (moving to valueB, my second highest value) and issueC (moving to valueD). Since it is late in the game (t = 0.8), I will accept these values to reward their movement. However, the opponent remains firm on their preferred values for issues B, D, and E (all valueE). To find a mutually acceptable compromise, I will concede slightly on issueB by moving to valueC, and offer mid-tier values on issues D and E, attempting to reach a deal with a utility around 0.58 for myself.

The opponent has recently conceded on issueA (moving to valueB), signaling flexibility, but remains steadfast on issueB, issueC, issueD, and issueE. Given the time fraction of 0.82, I will lock in their concessions on issueA, issueC, and issueE while trying a compromise position on issueB and issueD to maintain my utility around 0.53 to edge closer to an agreement.

The time fraction is 0.84, marking the late stage of negotiation. The opponent has made significant concessions on issueA (now at valueB) and issueC (now at valueD), while holding absolutely firm on issueB and issueE since the beginning. To facilitate an agreement, I will accept their positions on issueA, issueC, and issueE while trying to secure moderate value for myself on issueB and issueD, aiming for a total utility above 0.55.

The negotiation is approaching the deadline (t=0.86), and the opponent has consistently demanded `issueE: valueE` and `issueD: valueD` while holding firm on `issueB: valueE`. I am targeting a utility around 0.55-0.60 by accepting their concessions on `issueA` (`valueB`) and `issueC` (`valueD`), but expecting minor concessions from them on the remaining issues. I will propose a middle ground with `issueB: valueC`, `issueD: valueC`, and `issueE: valueA` to see if they can yield slightly on their core holdouts.

The time has reached 0.88, meaning we are in the very late stages of the negotiation. The opponent has made concessions on issueA and issueC, but remains completely firm on issueB, issueD, and issueE. Given the time pressure, I will concede issueD to their preferred 'valueD', but I will try to retain some utility on issueB ('valueC') and issueE ('valueB') to maintain an acceptable overall utility before dropping to my absolute minimum line.

The negotiation is in its final phase (t = 0.90), so I need to make significant concessions to reach an agreement while still securing a decent payoff. I see the opponent has made major concessions on issue A (getting me `valueB`) but is completely rigid on issues B, C, D, and E. I will fully concede on issues C, D, and E to their exact terms, and accept their offer for issue A, but I will ask for a slight compromise on issue B (`valueC`) to keep my utility reasonable.

The deadline is extremely close (t=0.92) and the opponent's bids have stubbornly converged on certain values, showing a solid refusal to compromise on issues B, C, D, and E beyond their current states, while moving positively on issue A. I will accept their proposal for A, C, and D, but attempt to secure a minor concession on issue B (to valueD) and issue E (to valueB) to maintain my utility around 0.5. If they refuse, this sets me up to accept their final demands just before the deadline.

The deadline is imminent at t=0.94. The opponent has made significant concessions on issueA (moving to valueB, which is highly valuable to me) but has remained entirely firm on issueB, issueC, issueD, and issueE. I will now concede issueE to their exact preferred value (valueE), while matching issueA, issueC, and issueD as they proposed. I will only step slightly back on issueB to 'valueD' to retain a marginal baseline of utility, signaling my readiness to close the deal.
